reasoning: The credit card debt series CCLACBW027SBOG has a historical upward drift in nominal terms. However, short-term trends show elevated recent numbers (reaching ~1056 vs the recent 1040-1050 range), which creates short-term random-walk volatility. The combined statistical estimate suggests a roughly 55-58% chance of short-term increase. As the horizon expands linearly to January, the natural upward drift is counter-balanced by the massive seasonal adjustment issues observed in January of the prior year (a ~40B drop from December 2024 to Jan 2025). Thus, we model a monotonically converging sequence: 0.58, 0.55, 0.52.
